items.title: Seed all RNGs once at startup
items.avoid: Scattering seed calls after data loading or model creation.
items.prefer: Single init function called at the very top of main() before imports that draw random numbers.
items.why: Any random operation before seeding consumes default RNG state and breaks repeatability.
items.example: def set_seed(s): random.seed(s); np.random.seed(s); torch.manual_seed(s) — call before load_data().

items.title: Hold splits constant during sweeps
items.avoid: Re-randomizing train/val partitions for each hyperparameter trial.
items.prefer: One random_state for splits and CV; vary only the knob under study.
items.why: Fair comparison requires the same evaluation set — Cross-Validation and train/val/test topics assume stable folds.
items.example: StratifiedKFold(..., random_state=0) shared across all models in a benchmark table.

items.title: Log reproducibility metadata with every run
items.avoid: Saving only final accuracy to a spreadsheet.
items.prefer: Log seed, git SHA, requirements lock or Docker digest, Python version, and hardware notes.
items.why: Future you (or a reviewer) needs the full bundle to rerun — Tracking Metrics shows how to attach params.

items.title: Report multi-seed variance when it matters
items.avoid: Publishing a single lucky seed as the only result for stochastic training.
items.prefer: Run 3–5 seeds for final benchmarks; report mean ± std when variance is non-trivial.
items.why: Seeds remove accident in development; multi-seed runs quantify stability for claims and papers.
items.example: Seeds {0,1,2,3,4} → val AUC 0.91 ± 0.008 instead of one run at 0.923.
